1989_10_06-India_Mumbai-Entering-The-Supreme-Mystery-SB_7_10_50-ST02_VR01

Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

Meta Data v8

Event Name/Title

Entering the Supreme mystery

Event Date

06 Oct 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Shreedhar Sahoo, Mansi, Reeshabh Bhat

Completed

01 Dec 2021

Source Audio

http://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-065-Entering%20The%20Supreme%20Mystery%20(Mumbai)-SB_7_10_50-dt_89_10_06.mp3

Source Video

NIL

Verse

SB 7.10.50

Tags – Places

Vrindavan, Dwarka.

Tags – Pastimes

Bhrama Vimohan Leela, Lord Krishna presence in Dwarka, The story of Ajamil.

Tags – Persons

Lord Shiva, Lord Bhrama, Yashoda mai, Nanda mahraj, Narada Muni, Lord Bhrama.

Tags – Themes

Humility, Love, Ego, Purification, Faith, Insignificance

Lecture Transcription

na yasya sākṣād bhava-padmajādibhī

rūpaṁ dhiyā vastutayopavarṇitam

maunena bhaktyopaśamena pūjitaḥ

prasīdatām eṣa sa sātvatāṁ patiḥ

[Sb. 7.10.50]

Translation

Exalted persons like Lord Śiva and Lord Brahmā could not properly describe the truth of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Kṛṣṇa. May the Lord, who is always worshiped as the protector of all devotees by great saints who observe vows of silence, meditation, devotional service and renunciation, be pleased with us.

Purport

The Absolute Truth is sought by different persons in different ways, yet He remains inconceivable. Nonetheless, devotees like the Pāṇḍavas, the gopīs, the cowherd boys, Mother Yaśodā, Nanda Mahārāja and all the inhabitants of Vṛndāvana do not need to practice conventional processes of meditation to attain the Supreme Personality of Godhead, for He remains with them through thick and thin. Therefore, a saint like Nārada, understanding the difference between transcendentalists and pure devotees, always prays that the Lord will be pleased with him.

Translation- Exalted persons like Lord Śiva and Lord Brahmā could not properly describe the truth of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Kṛṣṇa. May the Lord, who is always worshiped as the protector of all devotees by great saints who observe vows of silence, meditation, devotional service and renunciation, be pleased with us.

Lecture begins:

One great Vaishnav poet prays that he aspires not for heavenly planets and the pleasures therein, nor does he aspire for mukti, mystic sidhi, any earthly rewards but he only aspires to worship the lotus feet of Nanda Maharaja, in whose courtyard, the supreme absolute truth is appearing like a tiny child depending on his fatherly affection.

Krishna is Acintya. He is inconceivable. He is Acyuta, infallible. How to approach the absolute truth? As infinitesimal jivatmas, it is never within our capacity to understand in full the glo…glory of the absolute truth. Therefore, through the processes of transcendental meditation, through performance of our austerities, fasting, observing vows of silence, ha! By restricting the body according to very severe religious principles, by carefully studying the various philosophical documents and scriptures of the world, how is it possible from any of these processes for the infinitesimal jiva to understand the infinite absolute? Therefore, Krishna says in Gita to Arjuna that ‘To know me is a great mystery’ and through the ages there have been millions and millions and millions of persons, who in fact have carefully studied the various literate scriptures of the world including the Vedas and have performed very, very rigorous and disciplined spiritual practice and yet the absolute truth still remains a mystery before them. So Krishna tells Arjuna that ‘nathve aham’…um No!... ‘bhakto 'si me sakhā ceti’ rahasyaṁ hy etad uttamam [Bg. 4.3]. He says that ‘This ancient science is a great mystery, to understand Krishna is the deepest, most profound mystery of all secrets.’

rāja-vidyā rāja-guhyaṁ

pavitram idam uttamam

pratyakṣāvagamaṁ dharmyaṁ

su-sukhaṁ kartum avyayam

[Bg 9.2]

[This knowledge is the king of education, the most secret of all secrets. It is the purest knowledge, and because it gives direct perception of the self by realization, it is the perfection of religion. It is everlasting, and it is joyfully performed.]

The real spirit of Bhagvad-gita

It is the most secret of all secrets. Absolutely no one, has the power of entrance into understanding the mysteries of the absolute. It is only when the absolute being pleased upon us reveals himself to us causelessly that we can enter into these mysteries and the absolute only reveals himself according to the bhakti of the heart. Therefore, Krishna tells Arjuna, “Because you are my friend and my devotee, therefore you can understand the mysteries of this great science, no one else can do it.” There are so many commentaries on Bhagavad-gita. I remember one lecture Srila prabhupada was telling us that ‘Before he came to America that were almost one hundred editions of Bhagavad-gita translated into English language’ and he said ‘Scholars, professors, aspiring persons on the path of yoga and religion have studied these books very scrutinizingly.’ And yet not a single one of them became Krishna's devotee or understood the very obvious essence of Gita to surrender to Krishna, to always think of him and become his devotee. Prabhupada explains it is because practically all of these commentators of Bhagavad-gita has neglected to bring out the real spirit of Gita.

They all give their own interpretation and not a single commentator was a devotee of Krishna. So many Yogis, so many Swamis, so many professors, scholars, politicians, but not a single person who was a devotee of Krishna had ever translated Gita in English, until Prabhupada had done so and without a devotee one cannot possibly enter into the real spirit of the very essence of the Gita. Krishna did not say to Arjuna that ‘You can enter into this mystery of my understanding because you have controlled your senses very nicely or, because you are a very learned scholar or, because you are very eloquent in how you speak or because you have an infallible memory.’ He did not say ‘You can enter into the mysteries of this understanding because you are renounced, because you are very great.’ He said ‘You can enter into the mysteries of this understanding only because you are my devotee. Because you..you have humbled yourself before me and you are willing to surrender to my will.’ This is the only qualification.

Does Bhagavad-gita teach us everything?

Bhagavad-gita is a scripture specifically and exclusively bringing us to that conclusion of bhakti. It is a scripture for bhaktas. It is a scripture that only bhaktas can truly understand. Some less intelligent philosophers, they say that Bhagavad-gita teaches everything. It has section of Karma Yoga for the Karma Yogis. It has a section of jnana Yoga for the jnana Yogis. It has section of Astanga Yoga for the astanga-yogis. Bhagavad-gita has something for everyone. This is a great illusion. Bhagavad-gita commands us

sarva-dharmān parityajya

mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja

ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo

mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[Bg. 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

Bhagvad gita a scripture for bhaktas

Give up all these other varieties of religion and just become my devotee, surrender to me, that is the conclusion of Gita. That is the essence of all knowledge. Therefore, Srila Prabhupada said “If you want to give your commentary, if you want to tell your opinion then write your own book. But don't take Krishna's words from Bhagavad-gita and twist it and misuse them to suit your own purpose. Leave Krishna's, leave Krishna alone write your own book and you become famous.” But Bhagavad-gita is especially and exclusively a scripture for bhaktas, for humble bhaktas. Prabhupada said, “If you don't want to be a humble bhakta don't poke your nose in other people's business, leave Bhagavad-gita alone and write your own books. Give your own opinion your own interpretation.” Because it is only in reciprocation to the humility of a bhakta that Krishna reveals himself. And therefore, only in this way can one know Krishna in truth. Even the greatest personalities, great Devatas, great Yogis, the most exalted personalities in the universe, Narada muni is explaining, even they cannot properly describe the Supreme Personality of Godhead in truth.

The position of mother Yasoda in Krishna’s heart

But the residents of Vrindavan, headed by Nanda Maharaj, because of the simplicity of their devotion to the lord, the lord was always their protector. Not only their protector but he became even subordinate to them, simply to give them pleasure. Try to understand the position of someone like mother Yasoda. When Krishna is naughty, she picks up a stick [Indistinct]...thank him…. So, one may think what is this, how is this possible, he is the supreme God. She has such an intimate relationship with him that God is allowing her to hit him with stick. Do you know what an offense that would be for us to hit Krishna with a stick, but for mother Yasoda, when she hits Krishna with stick, he's very pleased, he's very happy. As a matter of fact, he'd rather be hit by her stick than hear her offering reverential prayers. It gives him more love. It gives him more pleasure.

But understand the background of mother Yasoda and her love for Krishna. Which is that background of all the residents of Vrindavan. That she is willing to suffer, to die, to go to hell at any second for Krishna. She has no consideration of her own comfort, her own welfare, her own happiness. She has sacrificed her life utterly for Krishna and Krishna alone. She's willing literally, not figuratively, she's literally willing to suffer in hell for the rest of eternity to give Krishna one moment of pleasure, if that was what was required. She has forsaken all selfish interests, all false ego, everything, simply to be the instrument for the pleasure of Krishna and it is because of this supreme sacrifice and renunciation, that she's entitled to hit Krishna with a stick.

What is the actual qualification of becoming the servant of Krishna

Until we are willing to die to live. Until we are willing to truly give up our own selfish interests utterly for the pleasure of Krishna, we are not given entrance into the realm of bhakti. But for one who was willing to do that. However, simple, unsophisticated or complex and sophisticated, however one may be that is not necessarily a qualification or disqualification, but that simple mood of surrender, devotion, is the greatest power in all of the creation because Krishna who is the all-attractive one, he agrees to be helplessly attracted to that humble devotion of his devotee and thus he is willing to become subordinate, to become the servant of that love.

Narada Muni was struck with wonder.

How does Krishna reveal himself to his pure devotee? Even the purest most elevated devotee cannot understand Krishna in full, that is not possible. But Krishna perfectly reveals himself in such a way that we could fully love him and we can fully serve him and surrender to him that is perfect knowledge of Krishna. Even Narada Muni, he is such a great devotee. He is on the highest platform of devotion and when he wanted to visit Krishna in Dwarka and he saw the sixteen thousand one hundred and eight palaces of Krishna. He began to visit one after another many of the palaces and each palace Krishna was doing something completely different. The mystic Yogis by the yogic siddhi, they can develop the talent by which they can expand themselves in up to eight forms at a time. But each of those eight forms, like a mirror reflection, must be performing the exact same actions as the other forms. And that is a very great yogic accomplishment which takes lifetimes of discipline to attain.

But Lord Sri Krishna in Dwarka in each house he was doing something unique. In one house, he was sitting on his bed speaking with his queen, in another, he was playing with his children, and another he was discussing the management of Dwarka with his ministers, in another, he was worshipping on the altar of the lord, and another he was giving charity away, in another he was taking bath. In each of these sixteen thousand one hundred and eight forms Krishna was uniquely engaging in different reciprocations in different relationships with his various queens and children and Narada muni was struck with wonder. It was just a tiny little glimpse of the inconceivable greatness of God. How even such a great soul as Narada he could only understand one thing at a time that Krishna was doing. He would go to each palace and he would see, ah Krishna is doing this here. But could he understand what Krishna was doing in all sixteen thousand one hundred and eight palaces at the same time? Krishna can understand every living entity, and what they are doing, and what they are thinking, simultaneously. Not only in this world, not only in this universe, but in all of existence. He is all-pervading aspect as the Paramatma. He knows everything about everyone at all times.

The eternally different and perfect vision of Sri Krishna

God is great. But even in the most elevated level of pure spiritual consciousness of transcendental realization, we cannot understand even what Krishna is doing in his sixteen thousand one hundred and eight palaces, in one little city of Dwarka. Even Narada muni could not. He had to go to each one to see, here Krishna's doing like this and there he…now, he's doing like this. Because we are jivatma. So, the perfection of Narada muni's vision, the perfection of the perfect jiva's vision, is eternally different than the perfect vision of Krishna.

vedāhaṁ samatītāni

vartamānāni cārjuna

bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni

māṁ tu veda na kaścana

[Bg. 7.26]

[O Arjuna, as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, I know everything that has happened in the past, all that is happening in the present, and all things that are yet to come. I also know all living entities; but Me no one knows.]

The perfect vision of Krishna is that he knows everything past, present and future. He knows all things. He knows all living beings. But Krishna says, but ‘No one could know him in full’, that is the difference. We can only know one little part, one little manifestation of Krishna, as he's revealing himself to us. We can understand in principle philosophically how God is great but as far as practical realization the ultimate realization is to simply love Krishna according to how Krishna is personally revealing himself to us.

The Gopis of Vrindavan, their perfection is that Krishna is revealing himself to them in such a loving way that their hearts, their minds are so captivated by that beautiful form, they cannot conceive of anything beyond it because factually there is nothing beyond it. It is the ultimate realm… realization within the beautiful form of Krishna, within his glance, within his smile, within his name, everything in all creation is contained, there is nothing beyond Krishna. Sometimes less intelligent philosophers they accept yes, it's very nice to worship Krishna but through the worship of Krishna we can realize what is beyond him, what is his source, but one who is a devotee of Krishna understands sarva karana karanam.[Bs.5.1] He is the cause of all causes. He is the source of everything. There is nothing beyond Krishna.

The original essence and source of everything

brahmaṇo hi pratiṣṭhāham [Bg. 14.27], the impersonal brahman is but a mere effulgence coming from his lotus like body. And in Isopanishad the prayer is my dear lord please remove this dazzling effulgence so I can see the beauty of your personal form. That personal form of Govindam as brahma prays, govindam ādi-puruṣaṁ tam ahaṁ bhajāmi [Bs. 5.30], is the original essence and source of everything. There is nothing beyond the form of God. Everything is emanating from the body of God and everything is within Him. Everything that exists is simply the radiant energy of the form of God the personality of God and the jivatma is just a tiny, tiny spark of Krishna's energy. But each tiny spark, by the greatness and glory of God has the capability according to his nature to have an infinite, complete and perfect relationship with Bhagwan Shree Krishna and it's only bhaktyā mām abhijānāti yāvān yaś cāsmi tattvataḥ [Bg. 18.55], it is only through the process of bhakti that Krishna reveals this relationship.

The process of sadhana is a process of purification

It is only through the process of devotion. Humility, devotion, surrender, that one by the mercy of the lord is given that knowledge. It is called siddha deha. The ultimate knowledge is to realize our eternal relationship with Krishna in the spiritual world, this is not something cheap. It is not that you approach a spiritual master and he simply tells you what is your relationship with Krishna in the spiritual world. He can tell you. Sometimes, they would ask Prabhupada, ‘What is our relationship with Krishna the spiritual world because there are many gurus who do tell.’ Prabhupada said, “What is it, it would be useless information even if I did tell you useless information. It would only be a disturbance to your mind. Until you give up all your material desires and develop prema bhakti, pure love, it will have no meaning to you.”

The understanding after the ultimate realization

But through the process of sadhana when we purify our heart and attain those progressive stages of love of God then gradually Krishna reveals to us from within our heart, our eternal relationship with him in the spiritual world. That of course is the ultimate realization, the ultimate satisfaction of the soul. And when that vision by the grace of Krishna comes upon us, we realize how infinitesimally small we are. Understand that this material world, this earth planet is just a little tiny, tiny particle in this universe and the Vaikuntha worlds and the ultimate planet of Vaikuntha, Goloka Vrindavan is millions and millions and millions of times bigger than this material world. And there are innumerable living entities and each one of them has his own unique intimate personal loving relationship with Krishna.

The journey towards the truth of Krishna consciousness

No one can understand everyone's relationship with Krishna. But Krishna relates, reveals your relationship with them. That is the difference between us and God. He is, he is Vibhu. He is all great and we are eternally anu, infinitesimally small, and the more advanced we become in truth in Krishna consciousness the smaller we feel ourselves. This is a fact. The more advanced you become in Krishna consciousness, the more you consider yourself an ignorant fool because as you see the truth of Krishna's knowledge. You realize that ‘I am an ignorant fool, I am nothing compared to Krishna. I have nothing to be proud of. All I can do is just be proud of Krishna because he is everything.’ But a neophyte devotee thinks he's intelligent, he's smart, thinks he's philosophical, thinks he's important, thinks he can do many wonderful things.

Lord Brahma was thinking that way and he's much better than any of us. Imagine, Brahma’s intelligence compared to ours. Ha! Can you imagine, how tiny your intelligence, you may have some degrees in college a master's degree, a PhD. But compared to Lord Brahma? He lives for the whole time of this…He lives for the whole span of his creation. By his intelligence, he has created all the planets and all the species of life within this world. Haa! what can you do? Compared to that? And yet brahma was thinking himself intelligent. He was thinking himself quite great because compared to every other creature in the universe, he is the greatest. Even compared to the demigods, compared to the demigods, Ha! we are we are foolish ignorant insects our life comes and goes in practically one breath of the demigods Ha! even the demigods they all worship and adore brahma as the greatest of the great.

Lord Krishna reveals a sample of his glory to Lord Bhrama

He is inconceivably superior in every respect to every single one of us and yet when he came before Krishna that beautiful little cowherd boy of Vrindavan dham, Lord Brahma was thinking that ‘My God, this Krishna is so great, that in ignorance of Krishna, I was thinking I'm very bright and it's like a firefly at midnight. In the darkness of the night, the firefly gives off light and thinks just see I am lighting all directions and the fact is in the middle of the night, moonless night when the firefly lights up his tail it appears my God! It’s so bright, so nice. But when the morning sun rises even if the firefly lights his tail as bright as he possibly can within his power. Nobody even sees him. Compared to the Sun, the firefly is so infinitesimal, so insignificant that he has no value whatsoever.’ Correct?

You are not the most anything

So, Lord brahma was thinking like that ‘because I have forgotten Krishna, I was thinking I was very great, very intelligent, very powerful, very important but now that Krishna has revealed just a tiny little sample of his glory I realize I am nothing, I am ignorant, I'm a fool, I'm insignificant. That's all I couldn’t say about myself as I'm totally insignificant.’ Just like the devotee who said, “Prabhupada, I'm the most fallen.” Prabhupada said, “you are not the most anything. you are simply insignificant.” [chuckles] That is our position, we are completely insignificant and the more we understand Krishna, the more we realize we're insignificant. A fool is a person who has insignificant knowledge. Correct? So, we are greatest fool. We are…. we can't even comprehend what fools we are. Ha! [chuckles] The more we are advanced in spiritual life not that the more we realize how learned we are. The more we realize what fools we are. The more we make spiritual advancement, it's not that we feel our selves more important the more we realize, we're completely unimportant. [chuckles] We're totally insignificant. We're important as a grain of sand on the passive beach or just one little tiny jiva.

The negative realization fuels the simultaneous positive realization of Krishna

But when we strip ourselves of all sense of prestige then our mind is no longer captivated by our own limited ego, then our consciousness can completely merge into loving appreciation of the supreme glory of Krishna and that is the ultimate happiness. Someone may think that if you feel yourself, totally unimportant, totally insignificant and a complete fool and how can you possibly be happy in life thinking like that? It is a fact that simply on that in terms of the negative realization there is no real happiness but that negative realization is the means of attaining the simultaneous positive realization of the infinite beauty and opulence of Krishna, who is the supreme most charming personality and when we take pleasure in his knowledge, his beauty, his wealth, his strength, his fame, his renunciation then our happiness, our bliss, our ecstasy, our love is infinite.

The faith of our heart is able to grasp the glory of God

So, this absolute truth, although it is sought after by different persons in different ways. He always remains inconceivable. Krishna is always inconceivable to everyone acintya shakti but to his devotees, although inconceivable he reveals himself in such a way that the more inconceivable, we understand he is the more we love him and he reveals himself to us in a personal relationship, which is simultaneously totally inconceivable and totally conceivable. Only Krishna can do like that. [chuckles] To try to understand this concept by material logic is impossible because factually Krishna cannot be understood by intelligence. Krishna cannot be understood by logic and reason. Krishna cannot be understood by any apparatus of our brain. Krishna can only be understood by our heart. Therefore, knowledge, philosophical conviction, learning learn learning Shastra. It is only approach, an approach which gives us a direction toward the path of Krishna consciousness, but it ends there. Ultimately it is the faith of our heart. The faith of our heart that is able to grasp the glory of God. It is never through our intellect or mind. Impossible!

The great mystery resolved

Bhakti means the heart. So therefore, through our learning of Shastra which is very important, to have that conviction to always stay on the right path and to properly discipline our life and through all of our activities in spiritual life. The goal is simply to purify the faith in our heart. So, that Krishna is pleased to reveal himself there and when Krishna reveals himself there, then there's nothing left to attain in life. Simply the aspiration for more and more and more and more service. The infinite never-ending thirst to love Krishna, to please Krishna is the true satisfaction of the soul. So, we can enter into this great mystery of understanding by Krishna's grace, by being the willing servant ready to sacrifice anything for his sake. Is there any questions?

Q&A Transcription

We are not proud of ourselves, rather our guru

[Devotee]: [Question is not clear] [43:00]

[Maharaj]: Hmm, that means you're in illusion. [chuckles] It’s only by the mercy of guru that you have anything. Is it your quality or the Guru kripa? You are not superior to the karmi. You are only more fortunate than the karmi, you are not superior, you are only more fortunate because guru kripa is upon you, you have accepted guru kripa that is your fortune. Ha! We should never think we are superior to anyone. We should simply consider ourself more fortunate that causelessly guru kripa has come into my life. So what do we have to be proud of? The only thing we have to be proud of is the glory of my guru’s kripa. We're not proud of ourself. We're proud of him. We're proud of the persons who have given us Krishna consciousness and we're proud of Krishna, who is working through them. But for us to be superior that's a horrible thought. Ha! [chuckles]

Never to think ourselves superior to anything

As soon as you think that I am superior, it is actually an insult to God because you're claiming what he is giving you to be yours, correct? Just like, if someone comes to a bank and you deliver that person ten thousand dollars. Are you superior to that person because you're giving the money? Is it your money? It's the bank's money right. You're giving a person a loan for ten thousand dollars. Are you giving a loan or is the bank giving a loan? Ha! So do you think yourself superior to that person because you're giving him ten thousand dollars. I have ten thousand dollars to give you. I'm superior to you. Does the loan agent think like that? If it was his money, he could think like that, I'm superior, I'm giving you the money but if you're just the loan agent and everything belongs to the bank. You're just thinking yourself ‘the humble servant of that person.’ You need the money, “okay! I'll arrange it, here it is.”

All what you have got is guru’s mercy

The loan agent is thinking himself the humble servant of the person he's giving the money to. Because he knows that in it not a single paisa is his. So, you may be giving knowledge to the karmis, but whose knowledge, is it? Is it your knowledge? You're just like a mailman delivering. As soon as you think it's yours you feel superior but as soon as you understand that it's all Krishna's, it's all my guru’s kripa. They can take it away anytime. They can give it anytime. So, therefore begging business is very humbling. When you accept as a beggar are you proud of yourself ‘Ha! [chuckles] just see, I've accepted this chapati, ha! very great, right?’ Does the beggar ever think? How can he think? The fact is that he's taking the chapati as a beggar, he's thinking himself ‘My god! I'm very low, this person is very great. He's giving me a chapati. But I'm not.’ So, when we accept the mercy of guru we're accepting like a beggar. We have no qualification. The more the Guru's mercy is given to you the more you feel completely humbled by that because you're like a beggar. An unworthy beggar with your arms, he's giving. There's nothing to be proud of. ‘Oh! I'm so great, I have accepted.’ No. “He's so great, he's giving.” Do you understand, no?

If you don’t humble yourself, you don’t really ever accept

[Devotee]: [indistinct]

[Maharaj]: That is our freewill. It is not that we, it is not that we don't have free will. It's not simply a matter of providence and destiny that has nothing to do with our freewill. It is, it is our freewill that accepts just like it's the freewill that someone comes to your door as a beggar correct and by his free will, he's getting your chapati correct but is he proud of himself that just see, “How great I am!” No, he feels himself humbled by being in that position as a beggar. Whereas someone who's not a beggar is very proud just see, “I have so many chapatis.” So, by your freewill you have humbled yourself to it. If you don't humble yourself, you don't really ever accept. It's all just a superficial show.

Accepting guru kripa is not just either you have it or you haven't. According to the quality of your humility to that degree you are accepting and according to the quality of your pride to that degree you're not accepting. You are superficially externally accepting just a tiny little fraction of what he's really giving. He's trying to give you. So yes, it is your free will and that is your it is your fortune that you're using your freewill in the right way and receiving the greatest gift which is causelessly far beyond you. It's hundred percent the Guru's qualification that you can make spiritual advancement. You're just like a beggar and you're just willing to accept it. Now certainly that's a glorious thing to accept it but it's nothing to be proud of. Should be proud in what you're receiving and from whom. Not in the fact that you have so gloriously decided to receive it. If you think like that, that means you've never received it. We should be proud of Krishna and proud of our guru. We shouldn't be proud of ourselves. Yes!

Krishna can break his own laws

[Devotee]: We can remember Krishna [distinct] guru kripa is also attained by being humble that I am accepting it. [Indistinct] [51:05]

[Maharaj]: Because the power of guru Kripa and Krishna's kripa is such that they can, they can break their own laws. Krishna can break his own laws. Somebody who's never really humbled themselves and never really undergone the discipline of sadhana properly by some inconceivable will of the lord, he does have the power to perfect that person. Ha! but that's very rare. Just like Prabhupada explains Rabindranath Tagore never went to college but he got an honorary PhD, a doctorate degree. Now if you think that ‘well I'm not gonna go to college. I'll just get a doctorate degree just like Rabindranath Tagore did.’ Do you think you will? He's one out of tens and millions who gave such a who have such a gets such a special concession of the lord so kripa siddha is like that.

Lord’s impartiality cannot be conceived by our rational understanding

[Devotee]: The lord is impartial; how can he be partial? [52:48]

[Maharaj]: Because he's not limited to your framework of what is possible and not possible for him. He's impartial but at the same time, he's inconceivable. [chuckles] So, his impartiality cannot be conceived of by our rational understanding. By principle, he's impartial but he’s inconceivable will. He can go completely beyond his irrational conception of what's impartial if he can't do that then he's not God. Correct.

Guru kripa means sadhan Siddha sadhan kripa

[Devotee]: [Question unclear] [53:54]

[Maharaj]: Guru-guru kripa means sadhan Siddha sadhan kripa, means by accepting the guru’s kripa in this form of his instruction and disciplining ourself in following his teachings, that is sadhana and perfecting our life through this process. Ultimately, it's not by our own attainment but we simply accept the mercy of guru through his teachings. We follow very, very, very carefully, very strictly, that means were accepting his mercy. And through the process of sadhana we attract Krishna to purify our heart, that is the standard process by which ninety-nine percent of persons attain self-realization but it may be that a person takes to the path like Ajamil he took to the path he was a very devout brahmacari. He was a very devout devotee but then due to his sensual impulses, he completely abandoned the spiritual path totally and became sinful wretch for practically all the span of his life trying because of association with the prostitute, he spent his whole life trying to fulfill her sinful desires. Now, Krishna who knows past present and future knew that he would be attached to his youngest son. So, Krishna within his heart caused him to name his son Narayan. Why? Because he knew he would call out that name at the time of death and he didn't even have any conception of God. When he was dying, he was… just out of family attachment call out the name Narayan Ha! Now that's called kripa sidhi.

What Krishna says is the absolute truth

Due to the past, though it was, he was sincere in the past and Krishna remembered his sincerity in the past. But somehow or other even though he completely left his sadhana behind he certainly wasn't a sadhana siddhi. He was a debauchee. By Krishna's inconceivable will, he wanted to be mercy upon that Ajamila. Why exactly? We cannot say he was partial. We can only understand that his impartiality in this regard. Krishna, Krishna says “He's impartial.” Right? Krishna says he's…we must accept when Krishna says “He's impartial”, he's impartial that's all there is to it you cannot argue the fact what Krishna says it correct? He's the absolute truth and what he says is the absolute truth. So, if he appears to be partial that's just what it is. It's inconceivable way of being impartial. [chuckles] ‘You'll never understand it you just have to accept it's inconceivable but it is impartial because he says he's impartial. Right. So why did he do that to Ajamil? Why doesn't he do it to everybody who's attached to prostitutes. Krishna has his reasons.

[Devotee]: So the what is the role of freewill then ?

[Maharaj]: In that case when kripa siddhi comes upon you you're just a very lucky man. [chuckles] Ajamil’s free will is when he saw the yamadutas tearing him by the throat back to the hell and he saw the Visnudutas protect him and he decided I'd rather go with them. [Laughter] Right. That was his free will [Laughter] but Krishna’s kripa siddha was he was so kind that he made it so clear to Ajamila that how could he possibly decide anything else,

but that mercy is very rare don't expect it. Don't become attached as prostitutes and sex life and think ‘ah! I will be like Ajamil.’ It's possible it can happen but it is not likely so it's better that you very diligently practice your sadhana and accept guru's kripa in that way. Do you understand? That means you've understood. Yes?

We are just imply delivering the knowledge of Krishna

[Devotee]: [Question unclear] [59:10]

[Maharaj]: Because you are representing your guru. When the mailman delivers to you a cheque for one lakh of rupees does the mailman think ‘I'm very rich. I'm giving you one lakh of rupees.’ But just because he doesn't he consider himself rich. Does that mean that he doesn't do his job? He's simply delivering. The knowledge is Krishna's knowledge. It's not your knowledge. You're simply delivering it as a peon you're simply delivering that knowledge with all force and all conviction.

If you want to be empowered: Give up the conceptions of I and mine

[Devotee]: [Question unclear] [1:00:10]

[Maharaj]: The conviction is not in us the conviction is in the power of our spiritual master and Krishna if you want to be empowered you have to give up the conceptions of I and mine that is the difference between a truly empowered preacher who can change people's hearts and someone who's a very good orator then maybe someone may be a very good orator and convince people's intelligence but then again politicians could do that, professors could do that correct? But to actually change people's hearts you have to be empowered and that empowerment comes to the degree we are the instrument in the hands of the previous Acharyas. It’s like when a doctor has an instrument what can the instrument do if it's not in the hands of the doctor the instrument does nothing all right it's the hands that it's in that makes the instrument glorious. The instrument has no glory in itself, correct?

So being humble doesn't mean we become ineffective. On behalf of our guru, we can be very bold, we can defeat all opposition as his instrument. We must preach very forcefully but we should understand who that force belongs to and we should just be the humble instrument in our heart. Any other questions? Do you understand now, yes?

Look in one place that when I sit on the Vyasa-asan and I'm accepting flowers glorification and worship from my disciples he said, “I feel like the dirtiest most useless dog sitting on this beautiful seat of God.” [Laughter] He's not feeling that oh I am the illustrious Acharya; he's feeling himself a dog. [Laughter] Does that mean he wasn't bold? Does that mean he wasn't convincing? Sometimes the dog barks very…. with great conviction[Laughter] It's the duty to his master right but he still considers himself a dog. [Laughter] Of course, we do not consider Bhakti Siddhanta Sarasvati a dog, we consider him the most illustrious empowered divine spiritual personality, but he's thinking himself a dog.

Accept the respect on behalf of your guru

‘Here I am… such a dirty dog like me on such a beautiful nice seat.’ I don't, he does… he wasn't thinking I deserve it I'm very great I'm very worshipful simply thinking that I am simply on behalf of Krishna I'm accepting this position… the more humble he becomes the more he thinks my god this is all Krishna's the more one glorifies a devotee. A devotee should be thinking ‘Lord Chaitanya he taught us not to be attached to any respect but how is it sometimes we have to accept respect.’ We're actually if we don't accept respect we're in in many ways we're abandoning our services as preachers. Correct? Because to engage people in Krishna's service we have to accept the respect sometimes we have to accept their praise but a devotee considers when the praise comes that I am so sinful and so…my heart is so dirty that these praise has nothing to do with me but because of the mission because of the mercy of guru that I'm representing this praise is actually going to that it's going to the mercy of guru that's coming that's coming through me as unqualified as I am and therefore it makes one almost embarrassed to…to conceive of accepting it himself when you're accepting on behalf of guru there's no embarrassment you're simply I just see ah! we're very embarrassed to accept anything for ourselves if we actually think we're great then really in trouble. Yes! what this said what this person is saying is very true he is very intelligent man and there's no disease that is we are diseased.Ha!

Never to make a grand show of one’s humility

‘Glories to our guru to the sadhus of Krishna’ that is the perfection of our life and the test see anyone can say that yes…yes! I am giving all glories to my guru, I am nothing, I am nothing. I'm the most fallen whatever you're saying I'm just giving to my guru anyone could say like that but the test is when you're criticized the test is when you're dishonored then do you fight back do you defend yourself or do you say yes…yes you are correct very good. Guru’s kripa is glorious, what you're saying about me I'm a lot worse huh I'm actually much worse than you think sometimes for preaching purposes we have to defend our position to some extent but in our heart, we should be thinking ha! actually he's only he's only his his blasphemous words of me is only touching the tip of the iceberg of my bad qualities right if you only knew the rest my God.

Huh, yeah that means that's all just so many empty words then as he's trying to be he's trying to get material recognition for being humble that's what it means it's not coming from his heart sometimes a show of humility is also an expression of pride. Just like one devotee. I remember this devotee very nice devotee actually very wonderful devotee but um even amongst very great devotees could be some subtle little tinges of pride that have to be corrected by great souls so this one devotee he would be giving class and they would want to give him not a big asana but they just wanted to give him a little cushion to sit on as the speaker of the class so he'd say “No no no they'd say “Yes please please!” “No no no I'm too fallen. I can't accept his cushion nobody else has the cushion” “but you're the speaker of the Bhagavatam” and just “No no no!” And because people thought he was such a good devotee they would just be sometimes. For the first ten minutes where there should have been class it was just like a little fight going on of whether to accept the cushion or not.

Sometimes two people would have to pick them up and put the cushion [chuckles] huh so what did this is not humility say if you were really humble you would just make the least show for yourself and just accept it but the fact you are making such a show of not accepting it is just you're trying to broadcast the glories of your humility. You just sit on the thing that's the most that's the with the least resistance that means you're just ..that means you really you're not concerned with trying to be humble you're just doing your service ha! So sometimes devotees not sometimes, very often humility is just to show to try to make oneself look very great very advanced. That's not humility, that’s just… that’s just a very…deceiving form of pride.

Being is humble is very tough

[Devotee] Unclear 1:12:04

[Maharaj]: Yes, they should be in that way he could preach to himself. [chuckles] At least he understands what are the real qualifications of a great soul. ‘The qualifications of a great soul is humility.’ At the beginning we may…we may to some extent pretend to be humble but through the process of purification, if we are sincere Krishna will put us through various situations of life where we get some understanding of what it really means to be humble and then we realize that: My God! being humble is completely something far different than I ever thought it was. I thought it meant just you know touching people's feet and saying I'm your servant but know what it means to be humble to be humble is a very, very hard thing. It's not just a superficial show it's something that has to come from the deepest core of your heart and it's very difficult. Until Krishna reveals gradually, your own imperfections.

It’s far more painful than death to really be humble

You have no idea of what it means to be humble but if you're sincere Krishna starts to reveal it to be humble means to be stripped of your false ego and you know how painful that is? To be stripped of your false ego it's more painful than death it's far more painful than death to really be humble that's why it is said ‘live we die before you die…die to live Ha! Die to live means the ego dies. It's far more painful than the death of the body it's millions of times more painful than the death of the body because your body, your bodies are dying on after another, after another, after another, after another it's no big thing to die from your body. It's painful we read about the pain of death it's horribly painful but it's happening all the time it's not very significant but when your ego dies then your material life is finished eternally. It's the ultimate death the death of the ego the death of the body is just a continuation of so many changes and it has to keep taking place until the ego dies so if death of the body is painful can you imagine how much death of the ego must be painful? Severe! We don't even know the extent of our ego we have no conception of the extent of our ego our consciousness of what would our sense of false pride and our sense of false ego our ego go so subtle it goes so subtle and so deep into our consciousness that all of the pride and all of the discussing matter of our false ego that we see is just the tiny little tip of the iceberg of our ego.

Krishna can lift our ego and toss it aside

Sometimes Krishna gives us a little glimpse under testing trying circumstances how much ego we really have how far we have to go and when we see it we're shocked then we realize, my God! it appears hopeless[chuckles] but our ego is like a mountain it's like there's certain mountains in the ocean they're like islands and those mountains go all the way to the bottom of the ocean and the top of the island is just the very surface of the tip of the peak of that mountain and we're playing on that and that's where we're ‘Oh that's all we see is that little peak but miles and miles and miles down it goes but we can't see it that's the way our ego is but Krishna can move mountains.’ Krishna lifted Govardhana Hill with the little finger of his left hand he can lift our ego and toss it aside if we take shelter of him. So really as you make advancement and become purified in Krishna consciousness Krishna puts you in painful conditions sometimes where he reveals to you a little more understanding of really the extent of your ego and then you have to understand that I am so fallen, I am so degraded I thought I was advanced but my God what an illusion to think I was advanced spiritually. [chuckles]

Realizing one’s insignificance and cry out for Giridhari

In the last twenty years I thought I was a very advanced devotee so many people are saying like this of me like God look what's in my heart it's horrible and in that condition, you have to just cry out for Giridhari, please come and remove this mountain, I have no other hope I have no other shelter than you and when you come to that realization then Krishna comes and moves the mountain and then you become and then you die. Then you have ultimately come to your material death and then your eternal life the realization of your eternal life with Krishna begins. So be prepared my friends many tests, many circumstances will reveal to you as you become purified how far you are from Krishna and in that condition, you'll do one of two things you'll either give up and say let me just be a karmi it's much easier on my ego[chuckles] or you'll just have to accept that you are the most helpless insignificant nothing and painfully admit it to yourself to others and to God and cry out for his mercy cry out.

Srila Prabhupada ki Jai! Thank you very much!

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
